---
title: "χc1(3872) Flatte model example"
output-file: index.html
engine: julia
---

This tutorial builds the default LHCb point-8 model, inspects its decay channels,
draws a basic lineshape, computes the pole position, and evaluates naive branching ratios.

```{julia}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
#| output: false
ENV["GKSwstype"] = "100"  # headless backend for CI

using X3872Flatte
using Parameters
using Plots

default_model = let
    @unpack Ef_MeV, g, Γ₀_MeV, fρ, fω = X3872Flatte.LHCb_point8
    FlatteModel(;
        Ef_MeV,     # MeV, binding energy
        g,          # Coupling to D*0D0
        Γ₀_MeV,     # MeV, decay width to other channels
        fρ,         # Coupling to J/ψρ
        fω,         # Coupling to J/ψω
        particle_data = ParticleData(),
    )
end
```

## Channel thresholds

Decay channels are stored on the model as typed channel objects.

```{julia}
#| label: thresholds
D⁰, D⁺, other, ρ, ω = default_model.channels
(
    neutral_DxD = threshold(D⁰),
    charged_DxD = threshold(D⁺),
    other = threshold(other),
    Jpsi_rho = threshold(ρ),
    Jpsi_omega = threshold(ω),
)
```

## Basic shape

The Flatte amplitude is evaluated as a function of the energy relative to the neutral
`D*0 D0` threshold. A minimal shape calculation needs only a model, an energy grid,
and `AJψππ`.

```{julia}
#| label: basic-shape-data
energies = range(-2, 2, length = 500) # MeV
intensity = [abs2(AJψππ(default_model, E)) for E in energies]

(
    peak_energy = energies[argmax(intensity)],
    peak_intensity = maximum(intensity),
)
```

## Pole position

```{julia}
#| label: pole
pole = pole_position(default_model)
(real = real(pole), width = abs(imag(pole)))
```

## Amplitude

```{julia}
#| label: amplitude-plot
#| fig-cap: "Squared amplitude |A|² for the χc1(3872) lineshape."
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
theme(:boxed)
plot(energies, intensity,
    xlabel = "m(J/ψπ⁺π⁻)-m(thr.) [MeV]",
    ylabel = "|A|²",
    title = "χc1(3872) Amplitude",
    fillalpha = 0.2, fill = 0,
    legend = false)
```

## Branching ratios

Naive partial widths integrated against the lineshape:

```{julia}
#| label: branching-ratios
(
    Jpsi_rho = X3872Flatte.dRρ(default_model),
    Jpsi_omega = X3872Flatte.dRω(default_model),
    DxD = X3872Flatte.dRDˣ⁰D⁰(default_model),
)
```
